Title: Schoolboy beaten by group in viral Tawau video sparks probe
Article:
TAWAU: A video showing a schoolboy being beaten by a group of individuals is under police investigation after the victim lodged a report.
The footage, which has gone viral since yesterday, shows a 14-year-old boy being assaulted by a group of school students.
In the incident at Jalan Sri Delima, Merotai Besar at 12pm last Friday, the victim and three friends were attacked while using a shortcut near an oil palm plantation on their way to Merotai Besar mosque for Friday prayers.
Acting Tawau district police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said a report was received at 8.09pm before the Criminal Investigation Division at the Tawau district police headquarters began investigations.
He said while passing through the area, the victim was approached by a group of school students known to him, who asked about the whereabouts of two individuals.
"However, one member of the group assaulted and punched the victim before several other students also joined in the attack.
"At the same time, the victim's three friends were also assaulted by the group," he said in a statement today.
He said the victim suffered injuries to the face, head, chest and ribs and was referred to Tawau Hospital for treatment.
The case is being investigated under Section 147 of the Penal Code for rioting, while further investigations are ongoing to determine the cause of the incident and identify those involved.
So far, no arrests have been made, and police are tracking down all suspects believed to be involved to assist investigations
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar turns to Tibetan ginseng to cope with hectic campaign schedule
Article:
KULAI: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has revealed his secret to coping with his packed Johor election campaign schedule — Tibetan ginseng.
Anwar said the gruelling campaign trail required extra energy as he moved from one programme to another with little time in between.
"Why am I still energetic? Because I take ginseng — Tibetan ginseng," he said, drawing laughter from the crowd at the Kita GenK Madani X programme.
The prime minister said his daily schedule had been packed with multiple engagements, sometimes stretching late into the night.
"I don't know how Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari arranges my programme. Today it's six events, tomorrow eight. He thinks I'm 30 years old," he said, referring to the PKR vice-president.
"They think I'm young. I'm already over 70, but they still treat me like I'm 30."
On some days, he said, he had as many as four programmes in a single evening.
"I look at the schedule — four, five, seven, eight events. And tonight, I have four programmes."
Anwar's schedule today includes rallies, dialogues and meet-the-people sessions in Kulai and Johor Baru.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: MCA seeks probe into alleged campaign material in Johor polls
Article:
JOHOR BARU: MCA has raised concerns over alleged defamatory campaign material published in a vernacular newspaper during the ongoing Johor election, claiming it amounts to propaganda and a breach of election laws.
The party said it would lodge a report with the Election Commission tomorrow, calling for a thorough and transparent investigation.
MCA secretary-general Datuk Chong Sin Woon said the material, published in Sin Chew Daily, contained statements urging voters to reject the party.
Chong also linked the material to rival political figures, which he said was defamatory and misleading.
He said the publication did not clearly identify the party responsible for the advertisement, raising questions over accountability.
MCA said the content could fall under Section 11(1)(c) of the Election Offences Act 1954 for improper election publications and campaign conduct.
Chong also criticised Pakatan Harapan (PH), accusing the coalition of resorting to "unethical" and "factually baseless" campaigning, and of relying heavily on issues such as the so-called "green wave" in its messaging.
"If that is all PH has to offer, then voters in Johor should reject them in this election," he told a press conference at the Johor MCA headquarters.
NST has reached out to Pakatan Harapan for comment.
He added that campaign conduct should remain clean and free from defamatory tactics, warning that such practices undermine democratic integrity.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 2 friends killed after motorcycles hit buffalo crossing road in Negri Sembilan
Article:
JELEBU: Two friends were killed after the motorcycles they were riding struck a buffalo crossing the road at KM11 Jalan Lakai-Simpang Durian at 3am today.
Jelebu police chief Superintendent Azizan Said said initial investigations found the crash involved two Yamaha Y15ZR motorcycles ridden by the victims, aged 17 and 19.
He said both were believed to be travelling from Simpang Durian towards Lakai when they collided with the animal as it crossed the road.
"As a result of the accident, both victims suffered severe head injuries and were taken to Jelebu Hospital for treatment.
"However, they were pronounced dead while receiving treatment at 4.05am," he said in a statement today according to Berita Harian.
Azizan said police were investigating the case under Section 41(1) of the Road Transport Act 1987, Section 289 of the Penal Code and Section 10 of the Minor Offences Act 1955.
"Members of the public with information on the incident are urged to assist investigations by contacting traffic investigating officer Sub-Inspector Abas Mohamed at 010-8649229 ...," he said.
Meanwhile, Kharizal Hamzah, 47, the uncle of Muhammad Aidil Zafuan Sameon, 19, said he met his nephew at about 9pm yesterday before leaving for Terengganu.
However, he received a call from a family member at about 3am informing him of the accident.
"Last night before I left, he came to see me. He even hugged me and asked where I was going.
"He was very close to our family, especially my second child.
"I never imagined our meeting last night would be our last, and I am deeply shocked by what happened," he said when met at the Forensic Unit of Jelebu Hospital.
The other victim was identified as Muhammad Faisal Fauzan Faizal, 17.
Both were laid to rest at the Kampung Lakai Muslim cemetery.
In a separate development, Kampung Lakai Village Development and Security Committee chairman Mohd Shahrir Ramli said numerous complaints had been lodged with authorities over buffaloes roaming along the road.
He said villagers had previously narrowly avoided accidents caused by livestock being allowed onto the main road.
"The animals also frequently trespass onto land belonging to the Orang Asli community nearby, and similar complaints have been lodged by local residents," he said.
Mohd Shahrir said the two friends had earlier spent time at the Kampung Lakai polling district centre before the accident.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysian caught smuggling 3kg of heroin in tuna tins at Macau airport
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A Malaysian man was detained at Macau International Airport last month for allegedly attempting to smuggle nearly 3kg of heroin worth MOP4.18 million (RM2.09 million) hidden in tuna tins.
In a statement, Macau police said the 24-year-old arrived in Macau on a flight from a Southeast Asian country late on June 20.
He was flagged by narcotics police at the airport.
"The man was detained after retrieving his checked baggage. An inspection uncovered 49 boxes containing tinned tuna and packets of biscuits.
"Each box contained two packets of biscuits and a tin of tuna. However, the tuna tins were found to contain a white powder later confirmed to be heroin," the statement said.
Police said the man was allegedly recruited by a Malaysian drug trafficking syndicate and promised RM3,000 for the job.
He was reportedly instructed to collect the drugs in another Southeast Asian country and smuggle them into Macau.
The drugs were intended for distribution in a nearby region.
Police have referred the suspect to the public prosecutions office for illegal trafficking in narcotic drugs and psychotropic substances.
In Macau, drug trafficking is considered a serious offence punishable by up to 15 years' imprisonment upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Early Johor polls no guarantee of bigger BN win
Article:
KULAI: Pakatan Harapan chairman Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim took a swipe at the Johor government's decision to dissolve the state assembly early, saying an early election was no guarantee of a bigger victory for Barisan Nasional.
"They think that by calling the election early, they will win more seats. Not necessarily," he said at the Kita GenK Madani X programme.
"The earlier they hold it, the more they may lose," he said, referring to the June 5 dissolution of the state assembly, about 15 months before its term was due to expire.
On the election, Anwar said the contest should focus on policies and improving people's lives rather than political infighting.
"Whether PH wins Johor or loses Johor, I remain the prime minister. I'm not saying this out of arrogance, but to remind everyone to be careful with what they say.
"What more do I want? I don't want projects for myself. I don't want to make more money. Our responsibility is to take care of the people."
Anwar also urged voters, particularly young Malaysians, to reject attempts to divide communities along racial lines.
"Don't accept those who want Malays to fight the Chinese, the Chinese to fight the Indians.
"This is our country. You have to protect our future."
He said Johor and Malaysia had immense economic potential, but prosperity must be shared fairly among all communities.
Anwar also defended his administration's record, saying it had increased salaries for civil servants and police personnel, introduced targeted fuel subsidies, and maintained one of the world's lowest petrol prices despite challenging economic conditions.
He acknowledged that not every problem could be resolved immediately but said the government was steadily implementing reforms.
Anwar said the federal government had channelled substantial allocations to Johor, including for major infrastructure projects such as the Johor-Singapore Special Economic Zone (JS-SEZ), the Rapid Transit System (RTS) Link and the proposed Autonomous Rapid Transit (ART) network.
However, he said development should not stop at mega projects alone.
"We can build highways, ports and rail projects, but we must also look after the people at the bottom.
"We must build more affordable homes, improve hawker facilities and help small traders because these are the issues that directly affect ordinary Malaysians."
He also urged voters to support leaders who prioritised public welfare over personal gain.
"Think about your children and grandchildren. This is your future, and this is your country."
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Fahmi: Puad's switch in support shows there's no such thing as fixed deposits
Article:
BATU PAHAT: No political party, including Barisan Nasional (BN), can currently regard any voter group as a fixed deposit, Pakatan Harapan (PH) communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said.
He was responding to a claim by former prime minister Datuk Seri Ismail Sabri Yaakob that non-Malay voters could no longer be considered a fixed deposit for DAP.
Fahmi pointed to former Umno supreme council member Datuk Dr Mohd Puad Zarkashi's recent declaration of support for the PH candidate in the Rengit state seat.
He said it was possible that more leaders and supporters could make similar declarations in the coming days.
"This proves that PH has its own appeal, which is evident particularly at the federal level. The leadership of Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim has projected a very positive image for the country," he said.
Fahmi added that several Bersatu members in the Sri Medan state seat had also shifted their support to PH.
He attributed the support to the country's economic resurgence, including in Johor, which he said had boosted confidence in the PH-led government.
"The political landscape has changed. Many have openly declared their support for PH, even though they come from different leadership backgrounds or political parties," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: PH dismisses Ong Kian Ming's forecast of BN landslide in Johor
Article:
BATU PAHAT: Pakatan Harapan (PH) has dismissed predictions by former Bangi member of parliament Dr Ong Kian Ming that Barisan Nasional (BN) will secure a landslide victory in the Johor state election.
PH communications director Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said Ong's forecast was inaccurate and far from reality.
"Dr Ong is my friend, and we used to argue a lot when he was a member of parliament. He has a right to his opinion, but it is incorrect and far from reality," he said after a meeting with Kampung Istana residents today.
Fahmi also rejected claims that Ong's remarks were linked to a delay in PH launching its manifesto, saying the document was unveiled at the right time.
He said PH chose to launch the manifesto only after its contents had been finalised and endorsed by the coalition's leadership, including Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim.
"For PH, we chose the timing, and once we felt the manifesto materials were complete, only then did we announce it.
"I think it was launched at the right time on Friday, during the second week of campaigning.
"So, there is enough time after introducing our candidates, and now both sides will showcase their respective manifestos," he said.
Yesterday, Ong predicted that BN could win up to 53 of the 56 state seats being contested.
The former deputy international trade and industry minister said the likelihood of BN securing a major victory had increased from more than 50 per cent to over 60 per cent after the first week of campaigning.
He said
the projection
was based on three scenarios favouring BN, PH and Perikatan Nasional, using voting data from the 15th General Election.
Under the scenario favouring BN, which he considered the most likely, PH was projected to win only the remaining three seats.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Chinese metaphysics forecast backs Onn Hafiz in Machap
Article:
JOHOR BARU: As Johor's election campaign intensifies, a Chinese metaphysics consultancy has entered the fray, predicting caretaker menteri besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi will retain Machap.
Kai Wee Consulting Group sparked discussion on social media after publishing a metaphysics-based forecast favouring Onn Hafiz's chances of holding the constituency.
In an Instagram post, the consultancy predicted that Onn Hafiz would successfully defend the seat, adding an unusual twist to the state election campaign.
Kai Wee Consulting Group is a feng shui and Chinese metaphysics consultancy offering astrology-based analysis and advisory services for individuals and businesses.
Born on March 2, 1979, Onn Hafiz is an Earth Goat in the Chinese zodiac.
"Within Chinese metaphysics, the sign is often associated with diplomacy, perseverance and an ability to build consensus," the post read.
The consultancy said its analysis of Onn Hafiz's birth date suggested characteristics traditionally associated with strong leadership.
The prediction drew interest online, with some welcoming the forecast while others viewing it as little more than election-season entertainment.
The consultancy's prediction came as Machap emerged as one of the closely watched battlegrounds, where Onn Hafiz is seeking another term against Pakatan Harapan's Nor Hafiz Roslan in a straight fight.
While political analysts typically rely on surveys, demographics and campaign trends to assess electoral prospects, astrology and feng shui practitioners have increasingly weighed in on major political events through forecasts rooted in traditional Chinese metaphysics.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor election triggers massive economic boom for local businesses, hotels
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Hotels, eateries, caterers, transport operators and small traders reported a surge in business as thousands of politicians, campaign workers and returning voters descend on the state.
With 172 candidates contesting 56 state seats, political parties have mobilised thousands of election machinery members from across the country, creating demand that stretches far beyond campaign ceramah and walkabouts.
Every constituency has become a hive of economic activity, from packed hotel rooms and fully booked homestays to bulk orders for meals, snacks, bottled water and campaign essentials.
At Gerai Wan Hamid in Nusa Perintis, fried chicken trader Abdul Aziz, 26, said demand has more than doubled since campaigning began — but the food still sells out faster than he can keep up.
"I doubled my supply, but everything is finished by around 1pm," he said.
"Before this, I usually close at 6pm. Now I pack up and go home by 2pm."
Nearby, fellow trader Zul Pikar, 27, said the lunch rush has become relentless as campaign workers, supporters and media crews flood the area in between political rounds.
The spike in activity is being felt across Johor's hospitality sector as well.
Malaysian Association of Hotels Johor chapter chairman Ivan Teo said occupancy has improved, particularly in Johor Baru and several urban centres, although the impact is uneven across the state.
"We understand the election is driving higher occupancy, especially in Johor Baru and nearby areas like Batu Pahat and Muar," he said.
"However, hotels in tourist destinations such as Desaru are seeing little or no significant impact."
At Amari Johor Baru, general manager Carl Nicholas Isaac said room occupancy has risen by about 40 per cent compared to the same period last year, largely driven by government-related bookings and campaign movement.
He said the influx had also boosted food and beverage sales, meetings and events, and other services, reflecting a broader spillover effect from the election period.
Novotel Johor Baru City Centre general manager Brendan Chong said the property had also recorded stronger demand following school holidays, with election-related travel adding further momentum to occupancy and local spending.
Shama Suasana Johor Baru hotel manager Syed Ali Syed Othman said the property had seen a sharp rise in long-stay bookings since late June, particularly from media teams and election support personnel.
"We have recorded about a 38 per cent increase in long-stay bookings," he said, adding that larger suite categories were in highest demand due to extended assignments.
Industry players say the surge extends beyond hotels, with catering, transport, printing and retail sectors also benefiting as campaign machinery moves across constituencies.
The New Straits Times noted elections typically create a short but broad-based economic ripple, as spending on accommodation, logistics, food and services filters down to small businesses.
For traders like Aziz, however, the impact is immediate.
"It's busy, but good business," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Super Typhoon Bavi forces emergency preparations in US Pacific islands
Article:
SAIPAN, Northern Mariana Islands: People in Guam and the Northern Marianas hunkered down on Saturday as the second "super typhoon" to hit the US Pacific territories since April approached with the equivalent of category-5 hurricane winds.
Bavi was several hundred kilometres east of the archipelago with sustained winds of 259km per hour, and gusts of 314kph, the Joint Typhoon Warning Center (JTWC) said in an update at 7am Guam time (2100 GMT Friday).
"Current forecast tracks continue to indicate a grim outlook for the Marianas," the US National Weather Service warned. "All residents across Guam and the [Northern Mariana Islands] should plan for and anticipate at least tropical storm conditions."
The islands' roughly 200,000 inhabitants have queued at petrol stations in recent days and thronged hardware stores to buy plywood and supermarkets for food, bottled water and other essentials.
Plans for celebrating the United States's 250th anniversary on Saturday have been quickly overtaken by storm preparations, with both Guam and the Northern Marianas declaring a state of emergency.
Workers at the Pacific Islands Club Saipan resort were boarding up windows, securing outdoor furniture and equipment, checking alarm systems and stocking first aid kits on Friday.
"Everyone has a role," the resort's sports, entertainment and activities manager Reymark Castro, 35, told AFP.
"From engineering to landscaping to operations, we're all working together to reduce damage and make sure we're ready to respond once the storm passes."
- STILL RECOVERING -
The Northern Marianas archipelago is home to around 40,000 people, and nearby Guam – a separate US territory – around 170,000. Major battles were fought in the area in World War II.
Super Typhoon Sinlaku, which hit in mid-April, knocked out power for tens of thousands of people, uprooted trees, overturned cars and ripped metal roofs off buildings.
A cargo ship, the MV Mariana, suffered engine failure and overturned in the storm. The body of one crew member was recovered and five others were missing, presumed dead.
"Some locals remain in temporary shelters or under makeshift roofs, while damaged seawalls continue to heighten concerns about storm surge," said the American Red Cross, which was deploying disaster teams and relief supplies ahead of Bavi's arrival.
Castro said he had sealed windows at his home with duct tape and filled up extra water containers.
"Two super typhoons in two months, in the same year, is a historic event," he said.
"Never before seen, for the Marianas at least."
The world's oceans experienced their hottest June on record and could set fresh highs in the months ahead, the European Union's Copernicus Marine Service said on Wednesday.
Warmer oceans help tropical storms to intensify and add more moisture, which can fall as heavy rain.
The onset of a potentially powerful El Nino weather pattern could boost global heat in the oceans and atmosphere even further in 2026 and into next year, scientists say.
The natural climate phenomenon warms surface temperatures in the central and eastern equatorial Pacific Ocean, bringing worldwide changes in winds, pressure and rainfall patterns.
The World Meteorological Organisation warned on Friday that El Nino, which typically occurs every two to seven years and lasts nine to 12 months, has already begun in the tropical Pacific.
The United Nations' weather and climate agency said El Nino conditions were forecast to "strengthen rapidly over the coming months, increasing the likelihood of... extreme weather events in many parts of the world."--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Gaza slips from global focus as Middle East war reshapes priorities
Article:
GAZA CITY: The Gaza war was the spark that touched off years of Middle East conflict culminating in the US-Israeli war with Iran, but as Washington and Teheran wrangle over terms for peace, the devastated territory's fate seems largely out of mind.
"Ever since the United States went to war with Iran, the whole world has forgotten Gaza and its tragedy. We no longer have anyone standing by us," Palestinian Ahmed Jamali, 53, told AFP from the displacement camp in Gaza where he lives.
"We are weak and oppressed, and Israel is doing whatever it wants: killing, destroying and occupying Gaza, while no one in the world lifts a finger."
The apparent inattention paid to the Palestinian territory is all the more striking because it sits at the heart of the chain of events that plunged the region into its most dangerous confrontation in decades.
Hamas's unprecedented Oct 7, 2023 attack on Israel triggered a devastating military response in Gaza, drawing in Teheran-backed allies like Hizbollah in Lebanon and Yemen's Houthi rebels – and eventually Iran itself.
What began as a local war between Israel and Hamas evolved into a regional conflict and, in turn, a direct confrontation between arch-foes Teheran and Washington.
More than two-and-a-half years later, Gaza remains mired in a severe humanitarian crisis, and despite a fragile ceasefire reached between Israel and Hamas in October 2025, efforts to bring the war to a definitive end have stalled for months.
Although Iranian officials initially spoke of an agreement to end the Middle East war that would encompass the entire region, the preliminary text endorsed by Teheran and Washington last month contains no mention of Gaza.
For analysts, that shows a shift in regional priorities.
"It reflects Hamas's declining strategic value in Iran's eyes," Hugh Lovatt of the European Council on Foreign Relations, told AFP.
Iran has long armed and financed Hamas as part of its "axis of resistance" – an array of regional forces opposed to Israel and the US – but the October 2023 attack appears to have fundamentally altered that relationship.
"Iranians do not really care about Gaza. Hamas was an ally, not an Iranian tool," said Israeli military expert Eado Hecht.
"It betrayed them. They did not want war in autumn 2023, it was too early for them."
- 'GRADUALLY FADING' -
Michael Milshtein, another Israeli military analyst, argued that Teheran's calculations have shifted elsewhere.
"It places greater value on preserving Hizbollah as a pillar of the regional balance," he said.
The diplomatic focus has also shifted, with a growing sense of international fatigue over Gaza.
"Gaza is gradually fading from international attention," said Lovatt.
One diplomat involved in negotiations described a widespread belief among governments that "most actors see the issue as insoluble in the short to medium term."
Another veteran diplomat based in Jerusalem told AFP that Gaza's absence from the discussions reflected political paralysis rather than progress.
"Gaza is absent from the agreement not because the war is over, but because no credible political framework exists for the day after," he said.
Israel insists that Hamas must fully disarm before any political transition can begin, while Hamas refuses to surrender its weapons without guarantees that an alternative Palestinian governing authority will replace it.
Neither an international stabilisation force nor a credible transitional mechanism has emerged in the months since the ceasefire took effect, both of which were called for in the US-brokered framework that halted the fighting.
- CAIRO TALKS -
Behind the scenes, negotiations over Gaza's future continue in Cairo.
The talks bring together Palestinian factions, including Hamas, alongside the Board of Peace set up by US President Donald Trump and regional players including Qatar and Turkiye.
"Trump may want to give this process a chance," said a source close to the negotiations.
"Whether it succeeds remains to be seen."
Although few details have emerged publicly, diplomatic and security sources told AFP that negotiators are working on a roadmap combining the gradual disarmament of Hamas with the creation of transitional governing authorities for Gaza.
Israeli media has reported that the government would reject such a framework.
"For now, this diplomatic process exists only around the negotiating table," Lovatt said.
"There has been progress, but reconstruction remains a distant prospect, and nothing is changing for the people on the ground."
- RETURN TO COMBAT? -
With diplomacy stalled, concerns are mounting that the fighting could yet resume.
Israeli media have reported military preparations for a possible summer 2026 offensive against Hamas should political negotiations fail.
But military expert Hecht cautioned against assuming that contingency planning meant another war was inevitable.
"Having the military opportunities is not the same as having the political opportunity," he said.
"Preparations are not the same as implementation."
Analyst Milshtein argued that Israel had little leverage left.
In his view, Washington could ultimately pressure Israel to accept a phased disarmament of Hamas alongside a transitional political framework – or even to withdraw from Gaza.
"Alternatively, Israel could embark on another military adventure. Given this government's record... (it) cannot be ruled out," Milshtein said, adding that Israeli leaders still lacked a coherent long-term strategy.--AFP
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sungai Muar holds treasure of unique white oyster ecosystem
Article:
MUAR: As early as 7.30am at Parit Tiram Jetty, the dark green current of Sungai Muar appeared calm and unassuming.
But beneath its murky depths lies a prized gastronomic treasure — white oysters, or Crassostrea (Magallana) saidii.
With the guidance of Parit Tiram village head Mohammad Daniel Azahari, 33, affectionately known as Tok Daniel, the writer was shown how the river's oysters are regarded as unique.
"Muar oysters are among the best in the world because they are rare white oysters. Many have tried to cultivate them elsewhere, but they did not survive. They need to grow naturally here," he said.
He added that he had never tasted the oysters before becoming village head, but was later impressed by their freshness and lack of fishy aftertaste.
Daniel said the oysters thrive due to the river's unique ecosystem, where saltwater flows in during high tide and freshwater pushes out during low tide.
"This creates a special environment where they live in both salt and fresh water," he said, adding that oysters transplanted elsewhere fail to survive.
He said harvesting remains fully manual, with divers collecting oysters from the riverbed without oxygen tanks, making it a risky occupation.
The geographical conditions of Sungai Muar also influence the oysters' flavour profile, with different beds producing varying sizes and textures.
Locals identify these areas with names such as Ngah Leman, Ngah Talib and Panjang shoals, each offering distinct characteristics shaped by silt, current and salinity.
In the river estuary, veteran diver Zaimi Mohd Noh, 57, continues the traditional practice of diving for oysters.
He began diving at the age of 13 and described it as a dangerous occupation requiring courage and resilience.
"This work is dangerous. If something happens on land, people can still find you. But in the river, it is different," he said.
"We keep reciting istighfar because we don't know what is happening below. Instead of looking around, we just dive," said the father of five.
He also said it has been difficult to attract younger people into the trade due to safety concerns and physical risks.
"We cannot force them. It has to come from their own interest," he added.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: China-Taiwan tensions rise after new coast guard patrol launch
Article:
BEIJING: China said on Saturday it had launched a new coast guard patrol east of Taiwan, drawing a sharp response from Taipei after a task force last month off the island's coast caused alarm in some Western capitals.
China's military operates almost daily around Taiwan, which Beijing views as its own territory. But China has begun using its coast guard to enforce its territorial claims in what Taiwan calls "lawfare" to claim a legal basis for Chinese actions.
The fleet will conduct "law enforcement patrols" in the area, China's Coast Guard said in a statement, adding it would strengthen such patrols in what it called China's jurisdictional waters.
The coast guard will "firmly safeguard China's territorial sovereignty and maritime rights and interests", it added.
TAIWAN CALLS NEW MOVE ILLEGAL, DISRUPTIVE
Taiwan's government condemned the new patrol, saying it was an "illegal expansion of power in violation of international law and a disruption of regional stability."
"The Chinese communists have no sovereignty or related rights in the waters east of Taiwan, have no jurisdiction over these waters, and none of its official vessels have any law-enforcement authority there," the Mainland Affairs Council, which makes Taiwan's China policy, said in a statement.
Taiwan's Coast Guard said it was tracking two Chinese ships and had prepositioned two of its own to sail alongside and monitor them.
As of mid-morning the two Chinese ships were 54 nautical miles east of Taiwan's Hualien, home to a major air base, but outside of restricted waters, it said in a statement.
Taiwan's Coast Guard will "continue to employ all necessary measures to forcefully expel Chinese vessels harassing our waters, steadfastly defend national sovereignty, and ensure the security of our maritime domains."
This is the second time in roughly a month that China, which views democratically governed Taiwan as its own territory, has sent coast guard ships into the waters off Taiwan's east coast. It risks escalating a diplomatic dispute that has drawn in the US, France, Germany and Britain.
China said the first operation in June was in response to an announcement by Japan and the Philippines that they would begin formal talks on their maritime boundaries, which Beijing viewed as involving Chinese waters off Taiwan.
Taiwan said on Wednesday that Taiwanese ships off the east coast should ignore any boarding and inspection demands by China's Coast Guard and, if necessary, Taiwanese Coast Guard vessels would intervene to stop it from happening.
China recognises no claims of sovereignty by Taiwan. Taiwan says China has no right to claim any sovereignty or jurisdiction over the island or its waters.
On Thursday, China's Ministry of Natural Resources published in English what it called a "legal opinion" on Japan-Philippines maritime boundary talks.
The two countries should have talks with China on the issue and not engage with Taiwan, the opinion said. "All other states shall refrain from providing assistance to Japan and the Philippines."--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Timber lorry driver arrested after testing positive for meth, ganja
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: A timber lorry driver was among two people detained after testing positive for drugs during a screening operation at the Gombak Toll Plaza today.
Kuala Lumpur Road Transport Department (RTD) director Hamidi Adam said the 31-year-old driver tested positive for methamphetamine and ganja.
The other detainee was a 41-year-old driver who provides car rental and chauffeur services, and who also tested positive for methamphetamine and ganja.
The pair were among 74 drivers screened by the National Anti-Drug Agency (Nada), which participated in the operation.
"Both drivers were detained under Section 3(1) of the Drug Dependants (Treatment and Rehabilitation) Act for further testing and investigation," Hamidi said, adding that their vocational driving licences would be suspended.
A total of 228 commercial vehicles were inspected during the operation, which involved the RTD, Nada, the police and the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry.
Hamidi said enforcement action was taken against 43 vehicles for various offences under the Road Transport Act. A total of 75 notices were issued, while a stage bus was seized.
In the same operation, Motac inspected 36 tourism vehicles and detected one case involving the failure to engage a licensed tour guide.
The case is being investigated under Regulation 6(1)(i)(iii), read together with Regulation 6(2) of the Tourism Industry (Tour Operating Business and Travel Agency Business) Regulations.
Upon conviction, the offence carries a maximum fine of RM50,000, imprisonment of up to five years, or both.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Boat capsize: Always wear life jackets at sea, says Terengganu exco
Article:
KUALA TERENGGANU: The Terengganu government has urged all participants in squid jigging (candat sotong) activities to wear life jackets at all times while on board boats, and advised skippers to conduct safety briefings before departure to reduce the risk of accidents at sea.
State Tourism, Culture, Environment and Climate Change Committee chairman Datuk Razali Idris said the wearing of life jackets should not be neglected, even when participants are resting or sleeping on board, as safety must remain the top priority throughout their time at sea.
"In my view, every boat carrying passengers must be equipped with life jackets, fire extinguishers and all the necessary safety equipment because we are responsible for people's lives. Whether they are crew members, passengers, or squid jigging participants, everyone must wear a life jacket at all times, including while resting or sleeping on board.
"This does not apply only to squid jigging activities but to all water-based activities. Life jackets must be worn properly and should not be removed until all passengers have returned safely to the jetty," he told Bernama when contacted today.
Meanwhile, Razali said boat skippers are also reminded to provide safety briefing covering safety procedures, sea conditions and the proper way to wear a life jacket before departing from the jetty or landing point.
He said the briefing should cover weather and sea conditions, the duration of the trip, and the importance of wearing life jackets properly.
"The practice should be adopted in the same way as tourist boats ferrying passengers to islands, which usually conduct safety briefings before departure. Skippers are also the ones who know the actual conditions at sea, which is why they need to brief passengers before the journey begins," he said.
He also reminded participants to heed the advice of boat skippers and not pressure them into proceeding with a trip if sea conditions are deemed unsafe, noting that most skippers in Terengganu are responsible operators who are willing to refund customers if a trip cannot proceed due to safety concerns.
Commenting on the boat that capsized last night, Razali said it was understood that the vessel had been in good condition before the incident and was operated by an experienced skipper,
although the actual cause of the incident
remains under investigation by the authorities.
"The sea was rough at the time, but there was no storm. It is understood that a large amount of water entered the engine compartment before the boat eventually sank. All the passengers were wearing life jackets at the time... That is why I stress that life jackets must be worn at all times and that everyone must not take safety lightly.
"They should keep them on throughout the trip, including while resting or sleeping on board," he said.
He said that the state government has no plans to revise the existing standard operating procedures (SOPs) for squid jigging activities, describing the current guidelines as adequate.
"Squid jigging has been carried out for many years. The existing SOPs are, in fact, sufficient. What is important is compliance with safety measures, including conducting safety briefings before the boat departs," he said.
He also reminded squid jigging operators and participants to comply with any warnings issued by the  Marine Department, including refraining from going out to sea if boats are prohibited from operating due to adverse weather conditions.
He said the state government could only issue advisories, as the licensing of boats and approval for their operations fall under the jurisdiction of the Malaysia Marine Department.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Govt targets 100pct internet access in populated areas
Article:
BATU PAHAT: The government through the Communications Ministry is targeting 100 per cent internet access in populated areas nationwide by next year through the implementation of the National Digital Network (Jendela).
Communications Ministry secretary-general Datuk Abdul Halim Hamzah said that as of Dec 31, broadband coverage in such areas had reached 99.71 per cent, which reflected the high commitment of the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC).
He said that before the implementation of Jendela, internet access nationwide was only around 90 per cent, but through the latest phase of the plan, MCMC will focus on Internet access up to 4G and 5G.
"The ministry and MCMC will continue to expand access to 4G and strengthen 5G, including in rural areas, which is being implemented through cooperation and infrastructure sharing between telecommunications companies.
"With this method, it can accelerate the expansion of coverage and improve the quality of services to users," he told Bernama after watching the live broadcast of the FIFA 2026 World Cup match in conjunction with Information Department's Arena Penggerak Komuniti (JAGO) programme in Kampung Istana, here today.
Also present to watch the match were the department director-general Erwin Khairul Ahmad and Kampung Istana Village Development and Security Committee (JPKK) chairman Abd Jalil Ali.
Halim said the expansion of good internet access can launch various sectors including education and the economy, especially in rural and interior areas.
Meanwhile, Erwin Khairul said the JAGO programme is a continuation of the 'wayang pacak' programme which is now being modernised, but its role is maintained as a delivery platform information and government policies.
He said that currently, there are four multi-function vehicle (MFV) units operating nationwide and his party plans to increase the asset to 16 units in the future, besides targeting each state to have at least one unit of the vehicle.
"We will always move throughout the country including delivering government initiatives such as targeted diesel subsidies, exemption from ATM withdrawal charges and book vouchers for students.
"In addition, the department also collaborates with the National Film Development Corporation of Malaysia (Finas) to broadcast local films to the community," he said.
Meanwhile, Jalil said as the host of the JAGO programme, his party was excited and at the same time proud when the village was selected as the Kampung Angkat Madani (KAM) of the ministry 2026.
"Through KAM programme, the residents here will benefit when the public hall built in 1950 will be upgraded with an allocation of RM400,000.
"In addition, another RM100,000 will be used by the local community for joint programmes and activities, including welfare contributions to the needy," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Houthis warn of strikes on Saudi airports over Iran flight
Article:
SANAA: Yemen's Houthis said on Friday its forces confronted Saudi "warplanes" that they said attempted to prevent an Iranian civilian aircraft from landing at Sanaa International Airport, according to a statement by the group's military spokesperson Yahya Sarea.
The Iran-aligned group warned that they would target "Saudi airports and vital interests on land and sea" if the Saudis continue what Sarea called violations of Yemeni airspace.
Flights between Sanaa and Tehran will continue despite any "possible consequences," he added.
On Friday, Houthi-run Al Masirah TV said that an Iranian aircraft arrived in Sanaa and flew the group's official delegation to Teheran to attend the funeral of Iran's late Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei. More than 200 patients were among other passengers on board.
Saudi Arabia led a military coalition that intervened in Yemen in 2015 after the Iran-aligned Houthis seized the capital, Sanaa.
Early on Saturday, the Saudi-led coalition said the statements by the Houthis against the Kingdom was an attempt to "divert attention away" from the group's "violations" against the Yemeni people.
It warned that it would respond firmly and with "unprecedented force" to any attempts to target the kingdom or violations to Yemen's sovereignty.
The Houthis have demonstrated their missile and drone capabilities during the Yemen war in attacks on Saudi Arabia targeting oil installations and vital infrastructure.--REUTERS
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: UKM denies claims that international students outnumber locals 5 to 1
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Universiti Kebangsaan Malaysia (UKM) has dismissed claims that its international students outnumber local students by five to one, saying foreign undergraduates make up eight per cent of its total student population.
In a statement today, the university said it currently has 46,151 students, of whom 3,917 are international undergraduates, based on the latest data from its Academic Management Centre and Centre for Continuing and Professional Education.
It said the actual ratio of international to local students is about 1:12, not the 5:1 claimed in a Facebook video.
It added that the admission of international students has not come at the expense of local students, adding that it remains committed to its founding mission of advancing education opportunities for Malaysians.
The university said international student enrolment is controlled and aligns with the Higher Education Ministry's agenda to internationalise Malaysia's higher education sector.
UKM also said it would not hesitate to pursue legal action against those who continue to spread defamatory or inaccurate information.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kangar local council seizes 400 units of liquid vape and 70 devices worth RM15,000
Article:
KANGAR: The Kangar Municipal Council seized 400 units of electronic cigarettes or vape liquid and 70 related devices, worth RM15,000, in an operation at a premises in Jalan Padang Behor here last night.
Its president, Affendi Rajini Kanth said the seizure, during the operation between 10.30pm and 1 am, in collaboration with the police, was carried out in accordance with the provisions of the MPK Trade, Business, and Industry Licensing By-Laws, in line with the Perlis state government's proclamation prohibiting the operation of vape sales premises in the state effective Aug 1, 2025.
"The premises in question has also been fined several times and a business cessation notice was issued in August 2025, yet it continues to operate the vape business," he said in a statement today.
He said that based on the provisions of the by-law, it imposes a fine not exceeding RM2,000 and an additional fine not exceeding RM200 for each day the offence continues after conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia aims to halve food imports by 2050
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: The government aims to reduce Malaysia's dependence on imported food by up to 50 per cent by 2050 in a bid to curb food imports, which are currently estimated at about RM80 billion annually.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the target would be achieved in stages, beginning with a reduction of at least 15 per cent by 2030, followed by 30 per cent by 2040.
He said the effort would be driven by various food security initiatives implemented by the Rural and Regional Development Ministry and its agencies over the past three years.
"Our goal is to help the country reduce food imports by 50 per cent from the current level.
"By 2030, I believe we can reduce imports by at least 15 per cent. By 2040, the reduction should reach 30 per cent, and I believe we can achieve a 50 per cent reduction before 2050.
"Although this is a long-term programme, based on the successes we have already achieved, we have a clear projection to reduce food imports through agencies that are not even under the Agriculture and Food Security Ministry," he said at a press conference after attending the Johor RISDA Agromajestic Programme 2026 at Dataran Kejat, Bandar Tenggara, here today.
Zahid, who is also the rural and regional development minister, said one of the key strategies was to utilise underused or idle land owned by government agencies for high-impact agriculture and livestock projects, including broiler chicken, layer chicken and beef cattle farming.
He said the initiative would not only increase domestic food production but also optimise the use of existing government assets to strengthen the country's food security.
"Land that is underutilised or not fully utilised will be converted for various Agro Madani agricultural projects to enhance food security, as well as livestock farming.
"This is being carried out in collaboration with various parties, including the Department of Veterinary Services, Felcra and other agricultural agencies under the state governments and the ministry," he said.
He said cooperation between ministries and agencies would help accelerate efforts to boost local food production, thereby reducing the country's reliance on imported food supplies over the long term.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Import ban on 5 Thai shrimp species stays for now
Article:
KOTA BARU: The import ban on five aquaculture shrimp species from Thailand will remain pending the  Fisheries Department's evaluation of data from Thai authorities.
Fisheries director-general Datuk Adnan Hussain said the assessment is expected to take at least 30 days, depending on the completeness of the data received from Thailand.
He said a technical team established by the department is scheduled to meet next week to review the second phase of information received before obtaining a more comprehensive overview of the country's compliance levels.
"For now, Malaysia is still maintaining the shrimp import ban from Thailand," he told a press conference at the East Zone 2026 myAgropreneur Perikanan (myAP) and myKomuniti Perikanan Carnival.
Adnan said the evaluation aims to ensure that all requirements related to biosecurity, disease control, and compliance with food safety standards are met.
On June 1, Malaysia put a temporary ban on five shrimp species from Thailand.
The temporary ban involves Penaeus esculentus (brown tiger prawn), Fenneropenaeus merguiensis (banana prawn), Penaeus vannamei (whiteleg shrimp), Penaeus monodon (giant tiger prawn), and Penaeus stylirostris (blue shrimp).
Adnan said among the main factors for the restriction was to ensure all shrimp imported into the country are free from any diseases that could threaten the local aquaculture industry.
"Apart from that, we also want to ensure that the imported shrimp do not contain any residues or prohibited chemicals that violate national food safety regulations and standards," he said.
He said Malaysia also faced a similar situation as local shrimp exports to Thailand have been blocked since 2017.
Adnan said the government is now streamlining the domestic control system to ensure Malaysian shrimp meet health and food safety standards, thereby opening the way for re-export to Thailand.
"We want to ensure Malaysian shrimp are disease-free and do not contain any residues or prohibited chemicals so that they can re-enter the Thailand market," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: BN-PH enjoy very good ties at federal level, says Zahid
Article:
KOTA TINGGI: Barisan Nasional (BN) enjoys a very good relationship with Pakatan Harapan (PH) at the federal level, with cooperation between the two political coalitions remaining strong despite the heat of the Johor state election campaign.
BN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi said the leadership of both BN and PH has consistently demonstrated professionalism and political maturity, placing the national agenda above all other considerations.
"Cooperation between BN and PH is very good. Let me confirm that it is indeed very good, and we remain highly professional.
"We distinguish between national issues, which we must address together as members of the Cabinet, and issues at the state level, for example," he said after launching the Risda Agro Majestic event in Bandar Tenggara today.
Commenting on the current Johor state election campaign, Zahid said both political coalitions remain committed to preserving harmony within the unity government by adopting a mature approach to election campaigning.
Zahid, who is also Umno president, said the political strategies of both sides are based on healthy competition without resorting to excessive provocation.
"Among the approaches we have adopted is that we do not attack one another, even though we are contesting in the same arena, particularly in Johor. The same approach will apply in Negri Sembilan, but we will not attack below the belt.
"If anyone from either side slips up, we should de-escalate the situation. Most importantly, democracy must be upheld and practised in the best possible manner," he said.
He said the winning formula in today's political landscape no longer depends on a party's ability to attack or discredit its opponents. Instead, voters assess the quality and merits of the candidates put forward.
He cited as examples the healthy competition among candidates with professional backgrounds, including in the medical field, as well as former ministers.
"We should promote our respective candidates, such as Dr Naqib Ghazali in Panti and Datuk Seri Dr Adham Baba in Pasir Raja, who is a medical specialist and a former health minister. These are the candidates we should be promoting, rather than attacking other parties or their candidates," he said.
A total of 172 candidates have been confirmed to contest the Johor state election, which is scheduled to be held on July 11, while early voting has been set for July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: Onn Abu Bakar outlines 6 pledges for Senggarang's development
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate for the Senggarang state seat, Onn Abu Bakar, today unveiled his 'Six Pledges for a More Progressive and Prosperous Senggarang' manifesto for the upcoming 16th Johor state election.
He said the pledges were comprehensively formulated and reflected his commitment to ensuring the holistic development of Senggarang, covering the economy, infrastructure, education, welfare and the well-being of the people.
Onn said the first pledge focuses on providing quality internet access for all by advocating improved telecommunications coverage in housing estates, villages and rural areas to support education, business and daily activities.
"The second pledge is to enhance public safety through the installation of additional village street lights and the upgrading of existing ones in identified high-risk areas to improve road safety, reduce the risk of accidents and help prevent crime," he said during a live broadcast on Facebook today.
On infrastructure, under the third pledge, he said efforts to upgrade roads and implement flood mitigation measures, including improvements to drainage and irrigation systems in Senggarang, would be prioritised to address flash floods and the impact of tidal surges, which have long affected residents in several areas.
The fourth pledge aims to strengthen the local economy through training programmes, access to financing and marketing support, as well as new business opportunities for entrepreneurs, traders, fishermen, farmers and smallholders to help increase their incomes, said the Batu Pahat member of parliament.
On youth and education, Onn said the fifth pledge seeks to expand opportunities for Technical and Vocational Education and Training (TVET), digital education and artificial intelligence (AI) training to produce a highly skilled workforce that meets current industry demands.
Under the sixth pledge, he said he would strengthen the role of the people's service centre to make it more responsive, efficient and accessible, ensuring that senior citizens, persons with disabilities (PwDs) and single mothers continue to receive assistance and are not left out of government welfare programmes.
Onn said the manifesto he is offering is not merely a collection of empty promises but a sincere commitment and a comprehensive action plan aimed at improving the well-being of residents in the Senggarang state constituency.
He said Senggarang has strong economic potential, given its rich agricultural and fisheries resources, vibrant small-scale industries and harmonious community.
He added that he would continue to push for the upgrading of fishermen's jetties so that they can be recognised by the Fisheries Development Authority of Malaysia, while also seeking to simplify the process of obtaining coastal fishing licences from the Fisheries Department.
On the Johor state election polling day on July 11, Onn said a voter turnout of between 70 and 75 per cent would give him an advantage and pave the way for victory in Senggarang.
Early voting for the Johor state election will be held on July 7.
N24: SENGGARANG
: 38,576 voters
1. Datuk Mohd Rashid Hasnon (PN-Bersatu)
2. Onn Abu Bakar (PH-PKR)
3. Mohd Yusla Ismail (BN-Umno)
(2022: Mohd Yusla Ismail: BN-UMNO: Maj: 3,912)
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Armed Forces to probe after 'distasteful' videos by 'soldier' go viral
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Armed Forces will investigate claims that a "soldier" has been uploading videos on social media accompanied by derogatory captions.
Armed Forces chief General Tan Sri Malek Razak Sulaiman said the military was aware of the posts, which have gone viral on Threads.
"We will investigate," he said when contacted, adding that the probe would determine the authenticity of the posts.
Earlier, a Threads user questioned the actions of the person behind the videos, which appear to show a man in military uniform accompanied by what were described as "distasteful" captions.
The post drew reactions from netizens, with many saying such actions could tarnish the image and reputation of the Malaysian Armed Forces.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Stick insects becoming harder to find amid concerns over habitat change
Article:
KOTA BARU: Stick insects, famed for their twig-like camouflage, are believed to be increasingly rare in the wild, sparking concerns over a potential population decline linked to habitat and climate change.
The Head of the Department of Preclinical Veterinary Sciences at Universiti Malaysia Kelantan (UMK), Dr Goh Soon Heng, said that although there is no scientific data indicating the species is under threat of extinction, early signs suggest the insects are becoming less commonly found than before.
"From a scientific perspective, the data does not show that stick insects are currently at risk of extinction. However, local observations suggest they are becoming increasingly hard to find compared with the past.
"If they are becoming harder to find, it may be an early sign that their population is declining, although more data is needed to confirm this," he told Bernama recently.
[[nid:1480265]]
He said ecosystem changes, rising temperatures, and shifts in plant species in their natural habitats were among the factors that could threaten the insects' survival.
He said changes in plants not only reduce food sources, but also impair the insects' ability to blend in and escape predators.
"When tree species in a given area change, the habitat and natural shelter of stick insects are also disrupted, making them easier for predators such as birds to detect and eat," he said.
He said Malaysia is home to more than 100 species of stick insects, varying in shape and size, with some resembling twigs while others resembling leaf insects, adding that the insects are mainly distinguished by their long, thin, and tapering bodies, which closely resemble real twigs.
"In addition to their bodies, stick insects have long, delicate legs, which make them hard to tell apart from twigs in their natural habitat," he said.
He said most stick insect species live in the tree canopy, with their location depending on size; larger species are usually found lower down, while smaller or younger ones tend to inhabit higher branches.
He said stick insects are neither poisonous nor venomous, although some species have spines on their bodies for self-defence.
The insects require between 10 and 15 months to complete their life cycle, from egg to a mature adult capable of reproducing, he said.
"The eggs usually take between two and three months to hatch before the young insects grow into adults within eight to nine months."
Dr Goh said stick insects play a role in the ecosystem by helping control leaf density across different tree layers and serving as a food source for a range of other animals.
He said this ecological role makes stick insects a vital component in preserving the balance of forest ecosystems.
He said no major conservation initiatives exist specifically for stick insects in Malaysia, although some zoos are involved in limited breeding programmes for stick and leaf insects to support education and awareness.
He also observed increasing public interest in stick insects as exotic pets, but warned against sourcing them from illegal channels that could affect wild populations.
"If protected species are involved, owners must obtain the required licences and adhere to regulations set by the Department of Wildlife and National Parks (Perhilitan)," he said.
He hopes that further research will be undertaken to determine the true status of stick insect populations in the country and to strengthen long-term biodiversity conservation efforts.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Lee: Selangor MB's Johor investment comparison oversimplifies economic realities
Article:
JOHOR BARU: A caretaker Johor executive councillor has defended the state's economic performance after Selangor Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Amirudin Shari claimed its investment inflows failed to generate enough local jobs.
Johor caretaker Investment, Trade, Consumer Affairs and Human Resources Committee chairman Lee Ting Han said data from federal agencies proved Johor's economic foundation remained solid.
Amirudin, who is PKR vice-president, said on the
campaign trail for the Johor polls
that the gap between investment growth and employment opportunities remained a concern.
Amirudin had said Selangor attracted RM83 billion in investments last year, creating 60,000 new jobs, compared to around 40,000 jobs created in Johor.
"With due respect, I believe this comparison oversimplifies how modern economies work," Lee told the New Straits Times.
He said Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA) figures showed Johor recorded RM110 billion in approved investments in 2025, the highest in the country, involving 1,348 projects expected to generate 24,584 direct jobs.
He said between 2022 and 2025, Johor attracted more than RM272 billion in approved investments, creating over 78,000 direct jobs, excluding indirect employment across construction, logistics, engineering and related supply chains.
The Barisan Nasional leader also cited Department of Statistics data showing Johor posted the highest Gross Domestic Product (GDP) growth in the country for two consecutive years, at 6.4 per cent in 2024 and 8.0 per cent last year.
He said Johor's strategy did not focus solely on job volume but on attracting high-value, capital-intensive industries.
These include semiconductors, artificial intelligence, advanced manufacturing, medical devices and data centres, which offer higher-skilled and better-paying employment opportunities.
Lee said this direction was aligned with the Johor Economic Transformation Programme, which comprises 83 catalytic projects and 32 strategic interventions aimed at creating more than 200,000 quality jobs between 2026 and 2030.
He added that initiatives such as productivity-linked wages and the Johor Talent Development Council were designed to ensure workers benefit directly from economic growth through higher skills and better income prospects.
Lee also questioned the focus of comparisons with other states, saying Johor's performance should be viewed within the context of its own economic trajectory.
"Johor is already Malaysia's top-performing state in terms of approved investments and GDP growth," he said, adding that continued collaboration between federal and state governments was key to sustaining momentum.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: [UPDATED] PM: PUNB targets RM2.25bil financing, govt may boost support
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Perbadanan Usahawan Nasional Bhd (PUNB) is targeting to approve up to RM2.25 billion in financing between 2026 and 2030 under its long-term R30 Strategic Framework to accelerate the growth of Bumiputera companies.
Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said PUNB has been performing well and indicated that the government is prepared to increase its support if the agency continues to deliver.
"They're doing well. If they do well, we will increase (financing approval).
"At the moment, PUNB is among the agencies implementing the priorities we have outlined, while working closely with other Bumiputera agencies," he said briefly after attending the SParK 2026: Business Transformation opening ceremony.
PUNB also announced a reduction in the financing rate for its Prosper Grow facility to as low as 3.5 per cent per annum.
The agency also unveiled three new financing programmes, namely, Prosper Grow Biz Express, Prosper Grow Fuel Up and Prosper Grow Auto Biz, aimed at improving access to financing and strengthening entrepreneurs' working capital.
The RM2.25 billion financing target will be achieved through PUNB's existing facilities — Prosper Grow, Prosper Great and Prosper Impact/Nova — alongside enhancements to programmes tailored to meet the evolving needs of entrepreneurs.
Held ahead of PUNB's 35th anniversary, SParK 2026 brought together PUNB Entrepreneur Partners, Bumiputera entrepreneurs, corporate leaders, industry players, digital ecosystem participants, development agencies and strategic partners for conferences, knowledge-sharing sessions, business exhibitions and sales activities.
The two-day event served as a platform for entrepreneurs to gain industry insights, build strategic networks, showcase their products and explore new business opportunities.
Since its establishment in 1991, PUNB has supported more than 15,500 Entrepreneur Partners through its financing and business development programmes, with total approved financing amounting to RM5.15 billion across various Bumiputera business sectors.
During the event, PUNB also exchanged memoranda of understanding with the Department of Statistics Malaysia (DOSM) and Malaysian Technology Development Corp Sdn Bhd (MTDC) to strengthen entrepreneur development through greater use of data, technology and innovation.
The collaborations are expected to improve PUNB's planning and evaluation of entrepreneur development programmes while creating more opportunities for entrepreneurs in technology, innovation and commercialisation.
SParK 2026 also saw five PUNB Entrepreneur Partners receive the SParK 2026 Entrepreneur Awards in recognition of their achievements, resilience and business discipline in building sustainable enterprises, creating employment, expanding market reach and demonstrating strong business leadership.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor campaign opens quietly as parties shift to targeted voter outreach
Article:
JOHOR BARU: The first week of campaigning in the 16th Johor state election has seen relatively moderate momentum, with all parties opting to engage voters more targeted rather than holding large-scale rallies.
Political analyst Prof Datuk Dr Sivamurugan Pandian said all parties are adapting their campaign methods to the current political environment through more personal approaches, including house visits, small group meetings and community programmes.
He said such strategies not only give candidates the opportunity to meet voters face-to-face and understand their concerns, but also help parties strengthen their machinery and make better use of campaign resources ahead of a more critical second phase.
"The first week is mainly about building the foundations of the campaign.
This targeted campaign approach actually gives candidates an advantage in reinforcing their position in the constituencies they are contesting," the Senior Lecturer in Political Sociology at Universiti Sains Malaysia (USM) told Bernama.
He said all party machinery has yet to reveal its real strength, and the campaign is expected to become more heated in the second week with senior leaders joining in, alongside more rallies and digital efforts targeting undecided voters.
Sharing a similar view, geostrategist Dr Azmi Hassan said modern election campaigns are increasingly data-driven, with parties also adopting hybridstrategies that heavily leverage social media platforms.
"In the past, campaigns were typically held on a large scale in open areas, but now campaign teams are deployed for direct face-to-face engagement to persuade voters, categorised as white, grey and black (supporters, neutrals and opposition)," said the Senior Fellow at the Nusantara Academy for Strategic Research (NASR).
Meanwhile, Ilham Centre researcher Mujibu Abd Muis said that, so far, campaign narratives from all contesting parties have largely focused on three main themes, namely, track records, future pledges, and efforts to convince voters that each party can deliver political stability.
The Universiti Teknologi Mara (UiTM) political science lecturer said, however, that these narratives have not yet formed a dominant theme strong enough to reshape the campaign landscape as a whole.
"Ultimately, the impact of any narrative depends on whether it can be turned into issues that resonate with voters' daily lives, including the cost of living, employment prospects, local development and public service standards," he said.
He said the first week also saw most of the party machinery concentrating its campaigning in northern Johor, covering districts such as Muar, Tangkak and Segamat, as well as parts of Batu Pahat and Kluang.
"In my opinion, this reflects how parties are optimising their campaign resources by prioritising constituencies regarded as competitive or likely to be decisive.
"Northern Johor has several constituencies expected to be closely contested, so the presence of national leaders is not merely to draw attention but also to signal their strategic importance to each party," he said.
Meanwhile, Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), Associate Faculty of Social Sciences and Humanities director Professor Dr Mazlan Ali said the campaign activity in Johor has so far largely focused on a "contest" between the two main coalitions, Barisan Nasional (BN) and Pakatan Harapan (PH).
Mazlan said PH is seen as having greater influence in critical areas such as southern and western Johor, while BN is expected to perform strongly in the eastern coastal districts, especially Mersing and Kota Tinggi.
"So, the way campaign machinery is mobilised is closely connected to how each coalition assesses its likelihood of winning in different areas," he said.
Meanwhile, all analysts agreed that parties must focus on persuading voters to turn out and cast their ballots, noting that voter turnout will have a major impact on the final outcome of the Johor polls.
A total of 172 candidates are contesting 56 state assembly seats in the state election, with polling day set for July 11 and early voting on July 7.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Tonton names 223 winners in first round of 'Non Stop, On Top!' campaign
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Tonton has announced 223 winners in the first round of its 'Tonton Non Stop, On Top!' campaign, although the identity of the grand prize winner, who will drive home a brand-new TQ Wuling Bingo EV, has yet to be revealed.
The grand prize winner will be announced during a prize presentation ceremony on July 7.
Tonton said winners in the runner-up, consolation and special consolation categories have already been announced, with 17 Oppo smartphones and 280 cash vouchers awarded across the three categories.
Following the conclusion of the first round, the second round of the campaign began on June 29 and will run until July 12.
Under the campaign mechanics, all points accumulated in the previous round have been reset to zero, giving participants an equal opportunity to compete for a fresh set of prizes.
To earn points, TontonUp subscribers must stream the following selected Korean titles on the Tonton platform during the campaign period:
- Exhuma
- Hijack 1971
- Dark Nuns
- The Moon
- The Priests
- Amazon Bullseye
- The Ghost Game
- Midnight Runners
- Dr. Cheon And The Lost Talisman
- Hopeless
- Peninsula
- Beautiful Audrey
- The Witch: Part 2. The Other One
- Revolver
- Sinkhole
- Confidential Assignment 2: International
- Concrete Utopia
- The Childe
- Emergency Declaration
- Decibel
The campaign's 'Turboost' feature also returns for the second round, allowing subscribers to earn double points by streaming selected content during weekends.
For the weekend of July 4 and 5, eligible titles are:
- Neraka Yang Terhijab
- Luka Di Hujung Setia
- Holy Night: Demon Hunters
- The Roundup: No Way Out

For the weekend of July 11 and 12, eligible titles are:
- Azure Spring
- I'm Calling Off the Engagement with the Male Lead
